channel.title: ProgressiveAutomation on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in ProgressiveAutomation on emsenn.net

item.title: Agent orchestration ecosystem survey (March 2026)
item.description:
This text surveys the agent orchestration ecosystem as of March 2026, assessed against the requirements of the agential semioverse tech stack: a Python service that loads skills from a library, exposes them via HTTP and MCP, and enforces a library/engine boundary where all admissible operators live in the library.

Frameworks surveyed

Claude Agent SDK (Anthropic)

The SDK that powers Claude Code. Provides a conversation loop, built-in file/shell tools, automatic context compaction, session persistence, subagent spawning, and MCP extensibility. As of early 2026 it includes “agent teams” (TeammateTool) for peer-to-peer messaging between agents, removing the parent-relay bottleneck of the subagent model. Locked to Claude models. MIT license (Python SDK).

item.title: Structured-work paradigms: a cross-disciplinary survey
item.description:
This text surveys structured-work methodologies across three disciplines — business/management, government/military, and scientific/academic research — to identify patterns that inform the design of agent workflows in the agential semioverse repository.

The survey was conducted via web research in March 2026. Each domain was researched independently to avoid cross-contamination of framing. Citations are provided per finding.

Cross-cutting patterns

Five patterns recur across all three domains. These are the findings most relevant to the emsemioverse’s workflow design.
